summary: Verified ADR 0279 from the latest realistic `origin/main` baseline at `0.177.133 / 0.130.83`, repaired the malformed Grist shared-edge OIDC discovery gate surfaced during the April 1 replay chronology, and completed a successful exact-main `r4` live apply that advanced the integrated platform baseline to `0.130.84` while leaving `grist.example.com` healthy and Keycloak login-gated.

verification.observed: The integrated tree passed `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python -m pytest -q tests/test_grist_runtime_role.py tests/test_grist_playbook.py` (`9 passed`), `ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/grist.yml --syntax-check`, `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards`, `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate`, and `uv run --with pyyaml python scripts/generate_slo_rules.py --check` before the final replay.

verification.check: Latest realistic origin/main baseline and committed source replay
verification.observed: `git fetch origin main` confirmed the latest shared baseline before the final replay was commit `5e9cfe6e528a4261bddfb9430417daf80d5b3be0`, which still carried repository version `0.177.133` and platform version `0.130.83`. The final governed replay from committed source `c1c75d88d10821e688731611632eb7334dd02042` is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-01-ws-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply-r4-0.177.134.txt` and ended with recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=309 changed=2 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=129`, `nginx-lv3 : ok=46 changed=4 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=7`, and `localhost : ok=24 changed=0 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=7`.

verification.check: OIDC bootstrap recovery path exercised end to end
verification.observed: The April 1 chronology preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-01-ws-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply-r1-0.177.134.txt` through `...-r4-0.177.134.txt` records the original blocked-login behavior, the one-off manual recovery in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-04-01-ws-0279-grist-oidc-bootstrap-manual-recovery-r1.txt`, the malformed embedded Python assertion surfaced by `r3`, and the final `r4` replay where both the shared-edge discovery gate and the targeted `grist` force-recreate after blocked-login detection succeeded automatically.

verification.check: Current public probes and in-container auth mode
verification.observed: Fresh post-success probes confirmed `curl -ksS https://grist.example.com/status` returns `Grist server(home,docs,static) is alive.`, `curl -ksS -D - -o /dev/null https://grist.example.com/o/docs/` returns `HTTP/2 302` to `https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3/protocol/openid-connect/auth?...client_id=grist...`, and `sudo docker logs --tail 200 grist 2>&1 | egrep 'OIDCConfig|loginMiddlewareComment' | tail -n 20` reports `OIDCConfig: initialized with issuer https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3` plus `loginMiddlewareComment: oidc`.

The exact-main replay completed the governed Restic follow-up trigger and synced `receipts/restic-backups/20260401T171418Z.json` plus `receipts/restic-snapshots-latest.json` back into the repo, preserving the post-apply configuration backup proof for the integrated platform baseline.
The successful replay still used `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` for this stateful service, and no new standby rehearsal evidence was recorded during the April 1 replay.

notes: Grist first became true on production during the earlier branch-local replay on platform version `0.130.60`; this April 1 receipt records the newer latest-main verification on top of the `0.177.133 / 0.130.83` baseline and the resulting integrated platform baseline advance to `0.130.84`.

The April 1 canonical receipt supersedes the March 31 mainline receipt `receipts/live-applies/2026-03-31-adr-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply.json`, while preserving that receipt plus the March 30 branch-local and March 30 mainline receipts as part of the audit trail.

The final replay started from the latest realistic `origin/main` commit `5e9cfe6e528a4261bddfb9430417daf80d5b3be0`, which still carried repository version `0.177.133` and platform version `0.130.83` before this promotion.

The manual OIDC bootstrap recovery recorded earlier on 2026-04-01 was preserved for auditability, but the authoritative proof for this promotion is the later exact-main replay where the repo-managed recovery path ran successfully without ad hoc runtime commands.

correction_loop.repair_action: Replaced the malformed inline Python OIDC discovery assertion with an environment-fed heredoc parser, added explicit curl connect and total timeouts, and replayed the exact-main Grist live-apply wrapper until the startup gate, blocked-login recovery path, public redirect contract, and Restic follow-up trigger all passed from committed source.
